message:
fix(clusters): show actionable error when kc-agent is too old to remove (#6288) (#6290)

The "Remove offline cluster" UI was surfacing "HTTP 404: Not Found"
when the kc-agent process was running but didn't expose the
`/kubeconfig/remove` route. Root cause: the route was added in
#5658 and users running an older kc-agent binary would get a
vanilla 404 from Go's http.ServeMux when the frontend hit it.

The generic message wasn't actionable — users (including @xonas1101
in #6288) couldn't tell whether the cluster name was wrong, the
context was gone, the agent was wedged, or the agent was simply
out of date.

Fix: detect response.status === 404 specifically and throw a
localized message that explains the likely cause and the remedy
("upgrade your kc-agent to the latest release"). Same scope as
the #6133 follow-up that surfaced 401 clearly.

Added i18n key `cluster.removeClusterAgentTooOld` to
`web/src/locales/en/common.json`. Other locales fall back to
English until translated.

Closes #6288

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2880,7 +2880,8 @@
     "removeClusterRemoving": "Removing...",
     "removeClusterSuccess": "Removed cluster \"{{name}}\" from kubeconfig",
     "removeClusterError": "Failed to remove cluster from kubeconfig",
-    "removeClusterNoAgent": "Local agent not connected"
+    "removeClusterNoAgent": "Local agent not connected",
+    "removeClusterAgentTooOld": "kc-agent does not expose /kubeconfig/remove. This route was added in kc-agent PR #5658 — please upgrade your kc-agent binary to the latest release."
   },
   "remediation": {
     "title": "AI Remediation Console",
